steps.title: Read the shown leg
steps.content: The segment shown has endpoints $(-6,4)$ and $(3,10)$. Its horizontal change is $3-(-6)=9$, and its vertical change is $10-4=6$.

steps.title: Find that leg length
steps.content: Use the distance formula: $$\sqrt{9^2+6^2}=\sqrt{81+36}=\sqrt{117}=3\sqrt{13}$$ So one leg of the right triangle is $3\sqrt{13}$ units long.

steps.title: Use the area
steps.content: Let the other leg be $x$. The area is $36\sqrt{13}$, so $$36\sqrt{13}=\frac{1}{2}(3\sqrt{13})x$$ Multiplying by $2$ gives $72\sqrt{13}=3\sqrt{13}x$.

steps.title: Solve for the other leg
steps.content: Divide by $3\sqrt{13}$: $$x=\frac{72\sqrt{13}}{3\sqrt{13}}=24$$ The other leg is $24$ units, which matches Choice B — $24$.
